name: Minecraft Modded Server (modpack)
description: Installs a Forge/NeoForge modpack from a direct "server pack" download URL (from CurseForge or a modpack's official page) and runs its bundled run.sh. Does not support Fabric/Quilt packs or Modrinth .mrpack files - see notes.

configFiles.description: JVM flags (edit this to change heap size - the vanilla MIN/MAX RAM variables don't apply here)

setupFiles.content:
# Generated by GameDock Manager. Setting this to true means you accept the Minecraft EULA (https://aka.ms/MinecraftEULA).
eula={{ACCEPT_EULA}}

# Generated by GameDock Manager. The server fills in all other settings on first start.
server-port={{GAME_PORT}}

variables.label: Modpack server pack URL
variables.description: Direct download link to the modpack's "Server Pack" / "server files" zip - on CurseForge, open the modpack's Files page, find the server pack download and copy its direct link. Re-running Update files with a new URL here updates the pack (mods from the old version aren't cleaned up automatically - wipe the instance first via the Files tab for a clean switch between unrelated packs).

variables.label: Accept Minecraft EULA (true/false)
variables.description: The server only starts when set to true. https://aka.ms/MinecraftEULA

variables.label: Game port (TCP)
variables.description: Written to server.properties at install. Auto-adjusted at creation if another instance already uses it.

notes: Only works for modpacks built on Forge or NeoForge - the vast majority of CurseForge modpacks - because it relies on the run.sh launch script they generate. Fabric/Quilt server packs (no run.sh, just a *-server-launch.jar) and Modrinth .mrpack files (a manifest that needs a resolver to fetch mods individually, not a ready-to-run zip) are not supported by this template. If run.sh isn't at the root of the extracted files, override the startup command in Settings once the Files tab shows you the actual layout. Memory is set via user_jvm_args.txt (created after first run), not a variable here - that's the file Forge/NeoForge itself reads.
